title: Lemon or Key Lime Curd Tartlets
ingredients: 2 tsp. grated lemon zest, or more to taste
4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces
¼ cup freshly squeezed key lime juice
instructions: For Lemon Curd: Strain the lemon juice to measure ½ cup or more.
Put the lemon zest and juice, butter, and sugar in a heavy bottomed non-reactive saucepan or double boiler over medium heat.
Heat until the butter is just melted and the sugar is dissolved.
Taste to see if you have enough sugar to balance the lemon juice.
Remove from heat and whisk the beaten eggs in a slow, steady stream, beating all the while.
Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon, 10 to 12 minutes in the saucepan or 15 to 20 minutes in a double boiler.
Remove pan from heat and stir to cool a bit.
Strain the curd and place plastic wrap on the surface.
Refrigerate the lemon curd.
For Lime Curd: Combine sugar, eggs, and lime zest and lime juice in a medium non-reactive heavy saucepan.
Cook over medium-low heat, whisking constantly until mixture thickens and holds the mark of the whisk, about 20 minutes.
Remove from heat and whisk in butter, one piece at a time, until well combined.
Strain the lime curd and place plastic wrap over the surface so skin doesn't form.
Chill the lime curd until serving.
For Almond Tart Crust: Cream butter and sugar thoroughly.
Add the ground almonds, egg yolk, almond extract, and flour, mixing well.
Chill the dough.
Grease 2" tartlet molds or spray well with non-stick cooking spray.
Press about 1 Tablespoon of dough into each mold.
Bake at 350 degrees for about 10 minutes, or until light golden.
Cool the tartlets in the tins, then carefully unmold.
Fill each tartlet shell with lemon or lime curd.
Top each tartlet with a dollop of fresh whipped cream, a berry, and a mint leaf.
notes: Lemon curd keeps in the fridge for about 3 weeks.
Don't boil or cook the lemon curd too long or you'll have scrambled eggs.
Almond tart crust yields about 36 tartlets.
If tartlet shells are not baked long enough, they will not release from the molds.
Tartlet molds must be re-greased or sprayed with non-stick cooking spray with each batch.
Handle cooled tartlet shells carefully as they break easily.
You can use either lemon curd or key lime curd for these tartlets.
